module.name: Announcements TTS
module.description: Plays back one of the system recordings (optionally allowing the user to skip it) and then goes to another destination, with automatic TTS generation via OpenAI. Add your OpenAI key to /etc/asterisk/openai.key after the installation. In shell paste below command (don’t forget to add your openAI key): touch /etc/asterisk/openai.key && echo "<YOUR_API_KEY_HERE>" > /etc/asterisk/openai.key

texts.description: You can give this announcement a friendly name for your own reference.
texts.text: Enter the text that you want spoken by the TTS engine.
texts.language: Select the language/accent for the TTS voice (e.g. “en” for English).
texts.voice: Choose which voice model (tone/style) to use for TTS playback.
texts.repeat_msg: Select a single DTMF digit that callers can press to replay the announcement.
texts.allow_skip: If “Yes”, callers may press any key to skip ahead immediately.
texts.return_ivr: If “Yes”, control returns to the parent IVR after playback instead of your destination.
texts.noanswer: If “Yes”, the channel isn’t formally answered – you get early‑media playback.
texts.post_dest: Where to send the call once playback completes (or is skipped).
